lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.instructions: Speak in a warm, confident, conversational tone. Keep pacing measured and clear with brief pauses.
lines.text: Hoy unimos tres temas: productividad, comunicacion y calidad tecnica.

lines.speaker: Lucia
lines.instructions: Speak in a bright, friendly, conversational tone. Keep pacing measured and clear with brief pauses.
lines.text: Por ejemplo, en productividad el bloque corto sirve solo si corta una espera concreta.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.instructions: Speak in a warm, confident, conversational tone. Keep pacing measured and clear with brief pauses.
lines.text: En comunicacion recomendamos acuerdos escritos y seguimiento semanal simple.

lines.speaker: Lucia
lines.instructions: Speak in a bright, friendly, conversational tone. Keep pacing measured and clear with brief pauses.
lines.text: Y en calidad tecnica, que riesgo compras si despliegas gradual sin metricas que te frenen.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.instructions: Speak in a warm, confident, conversational tone. Keep pacing measured and clear with brief pauses.
lines.text: Relacionar estos temas evita cuellos de botella y mejora resultados.

lines.speaker: Lucia
lines.instructions: Speak in a bright, friendly, conversational tone. Keep pacing measured and clear with brief pauses.
lines.text: Entonces escalar no vale mucho si cada equipo interpreta distinto lo que significa avance.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.instructions: Speak in a warm, confident, conversational tone. Keep pacing measured and clear with brief pauses.
lines.text: Cuando unes los tres temas, la clave es que cada uno baje un coste distinto del trabajo.

lines.speaker: Lucia
lines.instructions: Speak in a bright, friendly, conversational tone. Keep pacing measured and clear with brief pauses.
lines.text: Si no puedes nombrar ese coste, el marco queda bonito pero no cambia nada.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.instructions: Speak in a warm, confident, conversational tone. Keep pacing measured and clear with brief pauses.
lines.text: Y por eso conviene mirar el sistema entero, no optimizar cada pieza por separado.
